headline: US Semiconductor Sector Faces Anti-Dumping Investigation By China Ahead Of Trade Discussions
summary: In a move that could potentially affect the upcoming trade talks between China and the U.S., China has initiated two investigations against the U.S. semiconductor sector, including an anti-dumping probe.

headline: Bill Gates Once Signed A $1 Bill After Admitting He Was 'Wrong' And This Is Who Framed It For Memory
summary: Bill Gates handed over a signed $1 bill to concede an argument to Ravi Venkatesan during a visit to India, revealing his hard leadership style.

headline: SoFi Bets Big On AI With First ETF Launch In Two Years
summary: SoFi re-enters the ETF market with its Agentic AI fund, targeting next-gen AI leaders from Nvidia to Deere amid a crowded thematic investing space.

headline: Benzinga's 'Stock Whisper' Index: 5 Stocks Investors Secretly Monitor But Don't Talk About Yet
summary: The Benzinga Stock Whisper highlights five stocks seeing increased attention from readers during the week and a look at the potential reasons why interest is up.

headline: Steve Jobs Says Smart People Excel At This When Learning New Things, Science Shows He Had A Point
summary: Steve Jobs believed in connecting dots, supported by neuroscience. Intelligence is seeing the whole thing, not just being book-smart. Others like Bezos and Buffett also value learning and open-mindedness.

headline: 5 Stocks Investors Couldn't Stop Talking About This Week— Here's How They Fared: ORCL, OPEN, NBIS, UNH, AAPL
summary: Retail investors buzzed about 5 stocks this week, from Sept. 8 to 12, on X and Reddit's r/WallStreetBets: ORCL, OPEN, NBIS, UNH, AAPL.

headline: Benzinga Bulls And Bears: Oracle, Micron, Land's End — And Ellison Briefly Bests Musk
summary: Benzinga examined the prospects for many investors' favorite stocks over the last week — here's a look at some of our top stories.

headline: Howard Lutnick Says Elon Musk Got DOGE 'Backward,' Focused On Firing Instead Of Cutting Government Waste
summary: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said Elon Musk mishandled DOGE by focusing on mass layoffs instead of targeting government waste, leaving the program far short of its trillion-dollar savings goals.

headline: Palantir's 100x Revenue Multiple 'Inspired' Billionaire Marc Benioff, Maybe CRM Is 'Not Charging Enough,' Says Salesforce CEO
summary: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff praised Palantir's market positioning and premium pricing model, despite the size difference between the two companies.

headline: Tesla Faces Class Action Over 'Wage Theft' Amid Preference For H-1B Workers Following Elon Musk's Push To Optimize Visa Practice
summary: Tesla is facing a federal class action lawsuit accusing it of favoring H-1B visa holders over U.S. workers to cut labor costs, even as the company battles financial headwinds and regulatory scrutiny.

headline: Elon Musk's $1 Trillion Pay Package Is 'A Little Bit Weird' To Compare Monetarily, Says Tesla Board Chair: It's About 'Voting Influence'
summary: Tesla's board chair, Robyn Denholm, defended Elon Musk's $1 trillion compensation package, emphasizing its potential to drive unique performance despite recent sales struggles.
